file.content:
# frozen_string_literal: true

class MergeRequest < ApplicationRecord
  extend Gitlab::Cache::RequestCache

  include AtomicInternalId
  include IidRoutes
  include Issuable
  include Noteable
  include Referable
  include Presentable
  include TimeTrackable
  include ManualInverseAssociation
  include EachBatch
  include ThrottledTouch
  include Gitlab::Utils::StrongMemoize
  include LabelEventable
  include ReactiveCaching
  include FromUnion
  include DeprecatedAssignee
  include ShaAttribute
  include MilestoneEventable
  include StateEventable
  include Approvable
  include IdInOrdered
  include Todoable
  include Spammable

  extend ::Gitlab::Utils::Override

  sha_attribute :squash_commit_sha
  sha_attribute :merge_ref_sha

  self.reactive_cache_key = ->(model) { [model.project.id, model.iid] }
  self.reactive_cache_refresh_interval = 10.minutes
  self.reactive_cache_lifetime = 10.minutes
  self.reactive_cache_work_type = :no_dependency

  SORTING_PREFERENCE_FIELD = :merge_requests_sort
  CI_MERGE_REQUEST_DESCRIPTION_MAX_LENGTH = 2700
  MERGE_LEASE_TIMEOUT = 15.minutes.to_i

=== AfterCreateService ===
# frozen_string_literal: true

module MergeRequests
  class AfterCreateService < MergeRequests::BaseService
    def execute(merge_request)
      merge_request.ensure_merge_request_diff

      prepare_for_mergeability(merge_request)
      prepare_merge_request(merge_request)
      mark_merge_request_as_prepared(merge_request)

      logger.info(**log_payload(merge_request, 'Executing hooks'))
      execute_hooks(merge_request)
      logger.info(**log_payload(merge_request, 'Executed hooks'))
    end

    private

    def prepare_for_mergeability(merge_request)
      if async_pipeline_creation?(merge_request)
        logger.info(**log_payload(merge_request, 'Creating pipeline async'))
        create_pipeline_for(merge_request, current_user, async: true)
        logger.info(**log_payload(merge_request, 'Pipeline creating async'))
      else
        logger.info(**log_payload(merge_request, 'Creating pipeline'))
        create_pipeline_for(merge_request, current_user, async: false)
        merge_request.update_head_pipeline
        logger.info(**log_payload(merge_request, 'Pipeline created'))
      end

      check_mergeability(merge_request)
    end

    def prepare_merge_request(merge_request)
      event_service.open_mr(merge_request, current_user)

      merge_request_activity_counter.track_create_mr_action(user: current_user, merge_request: merge_request)
      merge_request_activity_counter.track_mr_including_ci_config(user: current_user, merge_request: merge_request)

      notification_service.new_merge_request(merge_request, current_user)
      merge_request.diffs(include_stats: false).write_cache
      merge_request.create_cross_references!(current_user)
      todo_service.new_merge_request(merge_request, current_user)
      merge_request.cache_merge_request_closes_issues!(current_user)

      Gitlab::InternalEvents.track_event(
        'create_merge_request',
        user: current_user,
        project: merge_request.target_project
      )

      link_lfs_objects(merge_request)
    end

    def link_lfs_objects(merge_request)
      LinkLfsObjectsService.new(project: merge_request.target_project).execute(merge_request)
    end

    def check_mergeability(merge_request)
      return unless merge_request.preparing?

      # Need to set to unchecked to be able to check for mergeability or else
      # it'll be a no-op.
      merge_request.mark_as_unchecked
      merge_request.check_mergeability(async: true)
    end

    def mark_merge_request_as_prepared(merge_request)
      merge_request.update!(prepared_at: Time.current)
      GraphqlTriggers.merge_request_merge_status_updated(merge_request)
    end

    def logger
      @logger ||= Gitlab::AppLogger
    end

    def log_payload(merge_request, message)
      Gitlab::ApplicationContext.current.merge(
        merge_request_id: merge_request.id,
        message: message

# frozen_string_literal: true

module Issuable
  class DestroyService < IssuableBaseService
    BATCH_SIZE = 100

    # TODO: this is to be removed once we get to rename the IssuableBaseService project param to container
    def initialize(container:, current_user: nil, params: {})
      super(container: container, current_user: current_user, params: params)
    end

    def execute(issuable)
      # load sync object before destroy otherwise we cannot access it for
      # deletion of label links in delete_label_links
      @synced_object_to_delete = issuable.try(:sync_object)
      # Capture assignees before destroy: the issue_assignees table has an
      # ON DELETE CASCADE FK, so after issuable.destroy the DB rows are gone
      # and issuable.assignees returns empty, preventing cache invalidation.
      @assignees_before_destroy = issuable.assignees.to_a

      before_destroy(issuable)
      destroy_ci_records(issuable)
      after_destroy(issuable) if issuable.destroy
    end

    private

    def before_destroy(issuable); end

    def destroy_ci_records(issuable)
      return unless issuable.is_a?(MergeRequest)

      issuable.pipelines_for_merge_request.each_batch(of: BATCH_SIZE) do |batch|
        ::Ci::DestroyPipelineService.new(project, current_user).unsafe_execute(batch.to_a)
      end
    end

    def after_destroy(issuable)
      delete_associated_records(issuable)
      issuable.invalidate_project_counter_caches
      @assignees_before_destroy.each(&:invalidate_cache_counts)
    end

    def delete_associated_records(issuable)
      delete_todos(issuable)
      delete_label_links(issuable)
    end

    def delete_todos(issuable)
      synced_object_to_delete = @synced_object_to_delete

      issuable.run_after_commit_or_now do
        TodosDestroyer::DestroyedIssuableWorker.perform_async(issuable.id, issuable.class.base_class.name)

        # if there is a sync object, we need to cleanup its todos as well
        next unless synced_object_to_delete

        TodosDestroyer::DestroyedIssuableWorker.perform_async(
          synced_object_to_delete.id, synced_object_to_delete.class.base_class.name
        )

# frozen_string_literal: true

module Gitlab
  module EventStore
    module Subscriptions
      class MergeRequestsSubscriptions < BaseSubscriptions
        def register
          store.subscribe ::MergeRequests::UpdateHeadPipelineWorker, to: ::Ci::PipelineCreatedEvent
          store.subscribe ::MergeRequests::ProcessAutoMergeFromEventWorker,
            to: ::MergeRequests::AutoMerge::TitleDescriptionUpdateEvent
          store.subscribe ::MergeRequests::ProcessAutoMergeFromEventWorker, to: ::MergeRequests::DraftStateChangeEvent
          store.subscribe ::MergeRequests::ProcessAutoMergeFromEventWorker,
            to: ::MergeRequests::DiscussionsResolvedEvent
          store.subscribe ::MergeRequests::ProcessAutoMergeFromEventWorker, to: ::MergeRequests::MergeableEvent
          # Only fire when no pipeline was produced. When a pipeline exists,
          # Ci::Pipeline.after_transition re-enqueues AutoMergeProcessWorker
          # on completion, so this subscription would be redundant.
          store.subscribe ::MergeRequests::ProcessAutoMergeFromEventWorker,
            to: ::MergeRequests::PipelineCreationCompletedEvent,
            if: ->(event) { event.data[:pipeline_id].nil? }
          store.subscribe ::MergeRequests::CreateApprovalEventWorker, to: ::MergeRequests::ApprovedEvent
          store.subscribe ::MergeRequests::CreateApprovalNoteWorker, to: ::MergeRequests::ApprovedEvent
          store.subscribe ::MergeRequests::ResolveTodosAfterApprovalWorker, to: ::MergeRequests::ApprovedEvent
          store.subscribe ::MergeRequests::ExecuteApprovalHooksWorker, to: ::MergeRequests::ApprovedEvent
          store.subscribe ::MergeRequests::ProcessDraftNotePublishedWorker, to: ::MergeRequests::DraftNotePublishedEvent
        end
      end
    end
  end
end

=== MergeRequestPolicy ===
# frozen_string_literal: true

class MergeRequestPolicy < IssuablePolicy
  condition(:can_approve) { can_approve? }

  rule { locked }.policy do
    prevent :reopen_merge_request
  end

  # Only users who can read the merge request can comment.
  # Although :read_merge_request is computed in the policy context,
  # it would not be safe to prevent :create_note there, since
  # note permissions are shared, and this would apply too broadly.
  rule { ~can?(:read_merge_request) }.policy do
    prevent :create_note
    prevent :accept_merge_request
    prevent :mark_note_as_internal
  end

  rule { can_approve }.policy do
    enable :approve_merge_request
  end

  rule { can?(:approve_merge_request) & bot }.policy do
    enable :reset_merge_request_approvals
  end

  rule { ~anonymous & can?(:read_merge_request) }.policy do
    enable :create_todo
    enable :update_subscription
  end

  rule { hidden & ~admin }.policy do
    prevent :read_merge_request
    prevent :update_merge_request
    prevent :approve_merge_request
  end

  condition(:can_merge) { @subject.can_be_merged_by?(@user) }

  rule { can_merge }.policy do
    enable :accept_merge_request
  end

  rule { can?(:admin_merge_request) }.policy do
    enable :set_merge_request_metadata
  end

  private

  def can_approve?
    can?(:update_merge_request) && is_container_member?
  end
end

# frozen_string_literal: true

class IssuablePolicy < BasePolicy
  delegate { subject_container }

  condition(:locked, scope: :subject, score: 0) { @subject.discussion_locked? }
  condition(:is_container_member) { subject_container.member?(@user) }
  condition(:can_read_issuable) { can?(:"read_#{@subject.to_ability_name}") }

  desc "User is the assignee or author"
  condition(:assignee_or_author) do
    @user && @subject.assignee_or_author?(@user)
  end

  condition(:is_author) { @subject.author == @user }
  condition(:is_assignee) { @user && @subject.assignee?(@user) }

  condition(:is_incident) { @subject.incident_type_issue? }

  desc "Issuable is hidden"
  condition(:hidden, scope: :subject) { @subject.hidden? }

  rule { is_incident }.policy do
    prevent :_read_assigned_work_item
    prevent :_read_authored_work_item

    prevent :_reopen_assigned_work_item
    prevent :_reopen_authored_work_item

    prevent :_update_assigned_work_item

I have everything I need. Let me present the complete audit.

---

# MergeRequest Contract Audit — gitlabhq

## Step 1 — Orientation

**Project:** GitLab — a Ruby on Rails monolith (community/FOSS mirror, `gitlabhq`). This checkout has **no `ee/` directory**, so all behavior lives in `app/` and `lib/`. It's huge, so all work below uses targeted lookups.

**Code organization:**
- `app/models/` — ActiveRecord models + `app/models/concerns/` (shared mixins)
- `app/services/` — business logic (command objects, namespaced e.g. `MergeRequests::`)
- `app/workers/` — Sidekiq async jobs
- `app/graphql/` — types, resolvers, mutations, subscriptions
- `app/serializers/` — JSON entities for the internal/web frontend
- `app/finders/` — query objects that enforce visibility
- `app/policies/` — Declarative-Policy authorization
- `lib/api/` — Grape REST API; `lib/gitlab/` — supporting libraries

**The central model — a proposed code change = `MergeRequest`** at `app/models/merge_request.rb:3` (a 106 KB / ~2600-line class). It is the spine much of the system attaches to. It is **built on shared concerns** (`app/models/concerns/`), the biggest being `Issuable` (`issuable.rb:9`) and `Noteable` (`noteable.rb`), and it `belongs_to` two projects (`target_project`/`source_project`, `merge_request.rb:43-44`). The query/visibility layer is `app/finders/merge_requests_finder.rb` over `app/finders/issuable_finder.rb`; authorization is `app/policies/merge_request_policy.rb` over `app/policies/issuable_policy.rb`.

## Step 2 — The MergeRequest contract and its foundations

**Concerns included** (`app/models/merge_request.rb:4-29`):
- `Issuable` (`merge_request.rb:8` → `app/models/concerns/issuable.rb:9`) — the big one; itself pulls in `Mentionable`, `Milestoneable`, `Subscribable`, `Awardable`, `Taskable`, `Participable`, `Editable`, `AfterCommitQueue`, `VersionedDescription`, `Exportable` (`issuable.rb:11-33`), and defines `author`/`updated_by`, `notes` (`dependent: :destroy`, `issuable.rb:56`), `label_links`/`labels`/`todos` (`issuable.rb:69-71`).
- `Noteable` (`merge_request.rb:9` → `noteable.rb`) — discussions/notes contract (`discussions`, `supports_suggestion?`, etc.)
- `Referable` (`:10`), `Presentable` (`:11`), `TimeTrackable` (`:12` → timelogs), `Approvable` (`:24`), `Spammable` (`:27`), `Todoable` (`:26`), `LabelEventable`/`MilestoneEventable`/`StateEventable` (`:17,22,23` → resource events), `ReactiveCaching` (`:18`), `ManualInverseAssociation` (`:13`), `ThrottledTouch` (`:15`).

**Associations a teammate must respect** (`merge_request.rb`):
- Projects: `target_project`/`source_project`/`merge_user` (`:43-45`)
- Diffs: `merge_request_diffs` (`:56`), `merge_request_diff`/`merge_head_diff` (`:67-71`), `merge_request_context_commits` (`:58`)
- Lifecycle/teardown records: `cleanup_schedule` (`:73`), `metrics` (`:78` autosave), `merge_data` (`:80`), `merge_schedule` (`:77`), `predictions` (`:74`)
- `events` (`:108`, `dependent: :delete_all`), `merge_request_issues`/`merge_request_closing_issues` (`:110,115`, delete_all), `user_mentions` (`:135`, delete_all)
- People: `assignees`/`merge_request_assignees` (`:131-132`), `reviewers`/`merge_request_reviewers` (`:133-134`), `reviews`/`reviewed_by_users` (`:146-147`)
- CI/deploy: `head_pipeline` (`:106`), `pipelines_for_merge_request` (`:127`), `deployments` (`:142`), `created_environments` (`:148`)

**Behavior contract:** two `state_machine`s — `state_id` (opened/closed/merged/locked, `:203`) and `merge_status` (`:255`); lifecycle callbacks `after_create :ensure_merge_request_diff` (`:164`), `after_commit :ensure_metrics!` (`:170`), `after_commit :expire_etag_cache` (`:171`), `after_save :save_merge_data_changes` (`:168`). **A teammate must know:** changing teardown semantics interacts with the three `dependent: :delete_all` (skip callbacks) vs `notes dependent: :destroy` (run callbacks), and with `Issuable::DestroyService` (below) which does extra deletion outside the model.

## Step 3 — Create / update flow and the fan-out

**Create:**
1. `MergeRequests::CreateService#execute` (`app/services/merge_requests/create_service.rb:5`) → `IssuableBaseService#create` (`app/services/issuable_base_service.rb:212`). `before_create` (`create_service.rb:46`) checks branches + spam.
2. `after_create` (`create_service.rb:23`) enqueues **`NewMergeRequestWorker` async** (`create_service.rb:30`) and marks MR preparing.
3. `NewMergeRequestWorker` (`app/workers/new_merge_request_worker.rb`) → **`MergeRequests::AfterCreateService#execute`** (`app/services/merge_requests/after_create_service.rb:5`), which fans out (mostly inline within the worker):
   - pipeline creation (`:19`, async or inline), mergeability check (`:58`, **async**)
   - `event_service.open_mr` (`:35`), `notification_service.new_merge_request` (`:40`), `todo_service.new_merge_request` (`:42`)
   - `create_cross_references!` (`:41`), `cache_merge_request_closes_issues!` (`:43`), diff cache write (`:40`), LFS link (`:53`)
   - `GraphqlTriggers.merge_request_merge_status_updated` (`after_create_service.rb`, subscription push)

**Update:** `MergeRequests::UpdateService#execute` (`app/services/merge_requests/update_service.rb:13`) → `IssuableBaseService#update` (`:309`) → `handle_changes` (`update_service.rb:25`): resolves todos, system notes, `todo_service.update_merge_request` (mentions), target-branch/draft-status handlers, label/assignee/reviewer/milestone diffs. Effects fan out via `execute_hooks` (web hooks) and the **EventStore** (`lib/gitlab/event_store/subscriptions/merge_requests_subscriptions.rb`) which routes MR domain events to async workers (e.g. `ApprovedEvent` → 4 workers, `:17-20`).

## Step 4 — Exhaustive dependent audit

### Models — `belongs_to :merge_request` (own a MR FK)
- `app/models/approval.rb` — records a user's approval of the MR
- `app/models/ci/pipeline.rb` — pipeline ran for the MR (`merge_request_id`)
- `app/models/clusters/agents/activity_event.rb` — agent event references MR
- `app/models/draft_note.rb` — unpublished review note on the MR
- `app/models/environment.rb` — environment created by an MR (`merge_request_id`)
- `app/models/deployment_merge_request.rb` — join MR↔deployment
- `app/models/description_version.rb` — versioned description belongs to MR
- `app/models/review.rb` — a submitted review of the MR
- `app/models/timelog.rb` — time-tracking entry on the MR
- `app/models/merge_request_assignee.rb` / `merge_request_reviewer.rb` — assignee/reviewer joins
- `app/models/merge_request_user_mention.rb` — cached @-mentions
- `app/models/merge_request_diff.rb` / `merge_request_diff_commit.rb` / `merge_request_diff_file.rb` — diff storage
- `app/models/merge_request_context_commit.rb` / `merge_request_context_commit_diff_file.rb` — context commits
- `app/models/merge_request/metrics.rb` / `approval_metrics.rb` / `cleanup_schedule.rb` — metrics + ref cleanup schedule
- `app/models/merge_requests/merge_data.rb` / `merge_schedule.rb` / `generated_ref_commit.rb` — merge state/scheduling
- `app/models/merge_requests_closing_issues.rb` — MR↔closing-issue link
- `app/models/resource_events/merge_request_assignment_event.rb` — assignment history

## Step 5 — Visibility & permission guards

Anywhere code acts on an MR, it checks `read_merge_request` (read) / `update_merge_request`/`admin_merge_request`/`accept_merge_request` (write) via `Ability.allowed?` → `MergeRequestPolicy`.

- **`MergeRequestPolicy`** (`app/policies/merge_request_policy.rb`) over **`IssuablePolicy`** (`app/policies/issuable_policy.rb:3`). It guards: `~can?(:read_merge_request)` ⇒ prevent `create_note`/`accept_merge_request`/`mark_note_as_internal` (`merge_request_policy.rb:14-18`); `hidden & ~admin` ⇒ prevent read/update/approve (`:32-36`, spam/banned authors); `locked` ⇒ prevent reopen (`:6-8`); `can_merge` (`@subject.can_be_merged_by?(@user)`) ⇒ enable accept (`:38-41`).
- **`IssuablePolicy`** supplies `locked` (`discussion_locked?`), `is_container_member`, `assignee_or_author`, `hidden` conditions (`issuable_policy.rb:5-20`) and delegates to the project (`delegate { subject_container }`, `:4`).
- **`ProjectPolicy`** is the real visibility gate: `merge_requests_visible_to_user` = `@subject.feature_available?(:merge_requests, @user)` (`app/policies/project_policy.rb:227-228`), which routes to `Featurable#feature_available?` → `has_permission?` (`app/models/concerns/featurable.rb:123,146`) checking the per-project MR access level (DISABLED/PRIVATE/ENABLED/PUBLIC). `merge_requests` is in the generated feature list (`project_policy.rb:259-261`), so `read_merge_request` is granted/denied through that machinery. `merge_requests_disabled | repository_disabled` ⇒ prevents all MR abilities (`:516-540`). `~merge_requests_visible_to_user` ⇒ prevents `read_merge_request_iid` (`:754`).
- **Finder-level visibility** (collection guard): `MergeRequestsFinder` (`app/finders/merge_requests_finder.rb:112`) over `IssuableFinder#execute` (`app/finders/issuable_finder.rb:119`) → `init_collection`/`filter_items` (`:133`) scope to projects the user is authorized for (via `by_parent`, override at `merge_requests_finder.rb:252`), so MRs from invisible projects never enter result sets — the visibility guard for list/GraphQL/API reads.
- **Closing-issues cross-check:** `MergeRequestsClosingIssuesPolicy` (`app/policies/merge_requests_closing_issues_policy.rb:8-11`) requires `read_merge_request` on the linked MR before exposing it.

## Step 6 — Blast radius (ranked by risk)

You are changing how the MR **stores and tears down attached data**. Risk ranking:

**🔴 Highest — teardown/destroy path (will break silently if mishandled):**
1. `app/services/issuable/destroy_service.rb:23-60` — MR-special-cased: `destroy_ci_records` (`:31`, batches `Ci::DestroyPipelineService`), `delete_todos` (`:50`), `delete_label_links`. Changing what hangs off the MR changes what this must clean up.
2. Model `dependent:` declarations: `events` `delete_all` (`merge_request.rb:108`), `merge_request_closing_issues` `delete_all` (`:113`), `user_mentions` `delete_all` (`:135`), `notes` `destroy` (`issuable.rb:56`). `delete_all` skips callbacks — if the rework adds child records that need callback cleanup, they'll orphan.
3. **Loose foreign keys** — async nullify/cleanup not visible in the model: `p_ci_pipelines.merge_request_id` (`config/gitlab_loose_foreign_keys.yml:687-689`), `vulnerability_feedback.merge_request_id` (`:1085`), `vulnerability_merge_request_links.merge_request_id` (`:1150`), `merge_request_requested_changes.merge_request_id` (`:544`); MR's own `head_pipeline_id` async_nullify (`:553-557`). A teardown rework must keep these consistent.
4. Ref/diff teardown: `cleanup_schedule` (`merge_request.rb:73`) + `MergeRequests::CleanupRefsService`/`DeleteSourceBranchWorker`; `merge_request_diffs` (`:56`) + `DeleteNonLatestDiffsService`/`MigrateExternalDiffsService`/`delete_diff_files_worker`.

**🟠 High — write services & async workers that mutate MR-attached data:**
`MergeRequests::{Update,Merge,PostMerge,Close,Reopen,Refresh}Service`, `Issuable::DestroyService`, approval services, and the EventStore subscribers (`merge_requests_subscriptions.rb`) — they assume the current association set and fan out async; an unannounced change to attached records can leave stale events/notes/todos.

**🟡 Medium — read/serialization contract:** `app/serializers/merge_request_*entity.rb`, `app/graphql/types/merge_request_type.rb`, `lib/api/entities/merge_request*.rb`, `lib/gitlab/hook_data/merge_request_builder.rb` — break consumers (web UI poll widgets, REST/GraphQL clients, webhooks) if a serialized field/association is removed or its teardown changes timing.

**🟢 Lower but verify:** finders/policies (visibility unaffected unless you touch project linkage), importers (`lib/gitlab/import*`), analytics cycle-analytics stage events, cross-model readers (`issue.rb`, `milestone.rb`, `deployment.rb`, `label.rb`).
